title: Problem-Solving Puppets
description: Cast two puppets, watch them hit a real kid-sized problem, watch every idea acted out live on stage, then tap the kind idea you like best.
learningGoals: See each solution to a conflict acted out before choosing — show, don't tell
Practice sharing, turn-taking, and asking for help with modeled kind words
Learn that unkind choices make friends sad — and that you can always try again
interactionModel: puppet-theater: live bone-rig puppets act the problem, act every choice as a preview vignette, then the child taps one large picture card to commit
feedbackModel: Kind choices play their acted happy ending with confetti and warm praise; unkind choices play a gentle sad beat, the scene snaps back, and the narrator invites a kinder try. No fail states.
credits: First shipping game on the shared bone-rig puppet engine. Puppet characters, acting clips, and voices from shared/characters/. Built with Claude for QLOBE Kids.
